era: 1989-1996 core. Grunge/Psych. E=7 -- heavy side of catalog. Fuzz as compression. Bass as primary weight structure. Slow tempos. Riff as hypnotic structure. U-District.
associated_musicians: Presidents of the United States of America (Jason Finn)

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 vs. E=5: same band, different emphasis. 221 = psychedelic haze. 325 = heavy fuzz compression. Two sides documented across two degrees.
theory_nodes.description: Love Battery's dual nature: documented at both E=5 (psych) and E=7 (heavy) in the engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Grunge/Psych fusion: heavy enough for Sub Pop, exploratory enough for psychedelia. Blue Cheer + Spacemen 3 + Black Sabbath genealogy. Not grunge directness, not psychedelic airiness.
theory_nodes.description: Love Battery occupies a specific genre position between grunge and psychedelia.

theory_nodes.concept: Fuzz as compression: at maximum density, the fuzz pedal creates sustained harmonic saturation rather than texture. Notes encased in overtones. The guitar becomes a wall.
theory_nodes.description: The technical distinction between E=5 and E=7 Love Battery guitar approach.

cross_references.relationship: E=5 first entry. The hazy airy side. Same band, same era. The 221-325 arc documents both modes.
Earlier return at E=7 grunge context. 325 is Grunge/Psych fusion specifically -- psychedelic DNA audible within the heavy structure.
E=7 holds. Sub Pop continues. Founding moment to return sequence.
E steps down to 6. Island Records. Capitol Hill. Post-summit energy descent continues.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=8. THREE KEYSTONE SPECIES simultaneously at the spawning creek. Most complex multi-species synthesis event in SPS. The heavy fuzz density of Love Battery against the density of three apex species competing and coexisting.
